$comment: Ch 4 — THE GLASS, anchor scenario (Duncan archetype). Authored from SCENARIO_SPECS_v1.md + SCENARIO_VOICE_v1.md (locked 2026-05-21). Per BASKETBALL_JOURNEY v2 §7, voice uses location + shot type ('FROM THE RIGHT ELBOW. OFF THE GLASS.'), never the athlete's name.

meta.title: FROM THE RIGHT ELBOW. OFF THE GLASS.
meta.subtitle: CH 4 — THE GLASS, ANCHOR
meta.authorIntent: The right-elbow bank. d=4.8, h_r=2.2, h_h=3.05. Textbook answer θ=50°, v=7.49 m/s. Verification: t = 4.8 / (7.49·cos 50°) = 0.997 s; y(0.997) = 2.2 + 7.49·sin(50°)·0.997 − ½·9.8·0.997² = 3.050 m — matches h_h (err 0.000 m). Slightly flatter arc (50°) — banks off the elbow benefit from a flatter trajectory that hits the square cleanly. Cultural framing is location + shot type, not the athlete — per BASKETBALL_JOURNEY v2 §7. Chapter payoff: the same square that confused the baseline shooter is the safest shot on the floor from this angle.

situation.questionRevealed: Find an angle θ and velocity v that lands the ball through the hoop.

subheadVariants.1: Came up short of the board.
subheadVariants.3+: Short again. Power well below threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Front iron. Aimed below the square.
subheadVariants.2: Still front iron. Aim higher on the board.
subheadVariants.3+: Front iron again. Push the angle higher.

subheadVariants.1: Back iron. Hit too high on the glass.
subheadVariants.2: Still back iron. Cut the velocity.

subheadVariants.1: Long off the square. Too much heat.
subheadVariants.3+: Far overshoot. Power well above threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Air ball. Missed glass and iron both.
subheadVariants.2: Air ball again. Aim for the square.
subheadVariants.3+: Still air ball. Check angle and velocity.

ghostArc.description: Textbook answer θ=50°, v=7.49 m/s. Controlled mid-arc — the boring shot that's geometrically optimal from the elbow.

hints.body: Bank shot from the elbow. Flatter arc is fine — the glass does the work.

Two equations, two unknowns:
  x(t) = v·cos(θ)·t
  y(t) = h_r + v·sin(θ)·t − ½·g·t²
Solve as a direct shot; the bank flavors the outcome.
Substitute t = d/(v·cos θ) into y. Try θ near 50° — lower than Ch 1 because a bank wants a flatter approach. Solve for v.

workedSteps: Solve y(t) = h_h for v → v ≈ 7.49 m/s.

intro.headline: FROM THE RIGHT ELBOW. OFF THE GLASS.
intro.subhead: Right elbow, hand in the face. The square is the widest target on the floor.

subheadByFlavor.GLASS: Hit the corner of the square. Fell.
subheadByFlavor.RIM_DROP: Square, then iron held it, then through.

miss.headline: OFF THE BOARD

diagnosticByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Front iron. Aimed below the square.
diagnosticByCategory.BACK_RIM: Back iron. Hit too high on the glass.
diagnosticByCategory.SHORT: Came up short of the board.
diagnosticByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Long off the square. Too much heat.
diagnosticByCategory.AIRBALL: Air ball. Missed glass and iron both.

bracketHintByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Geometry: aim higher on the square. θ near 50°.
bracketHintByCategory.BACK_RIM: Geometry: θ near 50°, cut v by ~0.4.
bracketHintByCategory.SHORT: Geometry: θ near 50°, push v near 7.5.
bracketHintByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Geometry: θ near 50°, cut v by ~0.7.
bracketHintByCategory.AIRBALL: Geometry: θ near 50°, v near 7.5 onto the square.

miss.afterAllHintsCopy: All hints revealed. The math is on the table.
